definition: Perceive the intended meaning of; grasp sympathetically.
example: You never really understand a person until you consider things from his point of view.

definition: Go up or into with effort; enter imaginatively.
example: You never really understand a person until you consider things from his point of view—until you climb into his skin and walk around in it.

definition: The natural outer covering of the body; a metaphor for another's life.
example: Until you climb into his skin and walk around in it.

definition: The ability to do something that frightens one; strength in the face of pain.
example: I wanted you to see what real courage is, instead of getting the idea that courage is a man with a gun in his hand.

definition: A weapon that fires projectiles; a false image of bravery.
example: I wanted you to see what real courage is, instead of getting the idea that courage is a man with a gun in his hand.

definition: An inner sense of right and wrong.
example: The one thing that doesn't abide by majority rule is a person's conscience.

definition: The greater number; the larger part of a group.
example: The one thing that doesn't abide by majority rule is a person's conscience.

definition: A songbird; a symbol of innocent beings who should not be harmed.
example: Shoot all the bluejays you want, if you can hit 'em, but remember it's a sin to kill a mockingbird.

definition: An immoral act; a wrong against the innocent.
example: Remember it's a sin to kill a mockingbird.

definition: A tribunal for justice; the arena of public judgment.
example: But there is one way in this country in which all men are created equal—there is one human institution that makes a pauper the equal of a Rockefeller, the stupid man the equal of an Einstein, and the ignorant man the equal of any college president. That institution, gentlemen, is a court.

definition: Having the same status, rights, or value.
example: But there is one way in this country in which all men are created equal—there is one human institution that makes a pauper the equal of a Rockefeller.
That institution makes a pauper the equal of a Rockefeller.

definition: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles.
example: Before I can live with other folks I've got to live with myself. The one thing that doesn't abide by majority rule is a person's conscience—and integrity is what remains when the town goes home.

definition: Unpleasant or repulsive, especially in moral character.
example: There's something in our world that makes men lose their heads—they couldn't be fair if they tried. In our courts, when it's a white man's word against a black man's, the white man always wins. They're ugly, but those are the facts of life.

definition: Easily understood; not complicated; plain in manner.
example: I do my best to love everybody—it's not simple, but it's the job.

definition: Be in or rise to an upright position; take a firm public position.
example: Scout, simply by the nature of the work, every lawyer gets at least one case in his lifetime that affects him personally. This one's mine, I guess—and a man must stand for it.
